fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Dakota Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity north dakota, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to north dakota, respiratory therapist compact north dakota, north dakota respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: North Dakota Board of Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address: 4202 E. Divide Ave., Suite 101, Bismarck, ND 58501-1198

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in North Dakota.

steps.title: Create a Licensure Gateway Account
steps.description: Register as a new user on the North Dakota Board of Medical Examiners Licensure Gateway portal to access the application system.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Licensure Gateway, select the Respiratory Care Practitioner application, and accurately fill out all required sections.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for submission of official transcripts from an accredited respiratory care program and verification of your National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) RRT credential. Additional documents, such as a consent form for a background check, may be required.

steps.title: Undergo Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required fingerprinting for a criminal history record check through the North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) and the FBI. Specific instructions will be provided within the application process.

steps.title: Pay Application and Licensure Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee ($150) and the initial licensure fee ($100) through the Licensure Gateway portal. A separate fee ($41.25) is paid directly to the fingerprint vendor.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your Licensure Gateway account for any deficiencies or requests for additional information from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Dakota respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in North Dakota. To work in North Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in North Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in North Dakota, applicants must complete the online application through the North Dakota Board of Medical Examiners' Licensure Gateway portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Licensure Gateway portal, click 'New User Registration', and follow the prompts to create an account with your email and a password. Once registered, you can log in to begin your application.
